---
title: "stwd_color"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L127)

# `stwd_color()`

Color Text with Marquee Syntax

## Usage

```r
stwd_color(color, text, bold = FALSE)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `color` | A color specified as hex code (e.g., "#E69F00") or named R color (e.g., "midnightblue", "steelblue", "forestgreen"). |
| `text` | The text to color. |
| `bold` | Logical; wrap text in bold? Default: FALSE. |

## Value

A marquee-formatted string for use in title, subtitle, or narrative.

## Description

A helper function to apply color to text using marquee syntax.
Accepts both hex codes (e.g., "#191970") and named R colors
(e.g., "midnightblue").

## Examples

```r
# Using hex code
stwd_color("#E69F00", "highlighted text")

# Using named color
stwd_color("midnightblue", "North region")

# With bold
stwd_color("steelblue", "important", bold = TRUE)

# In a title
title <- paste0("Sales in ", stwd_color("forestgreen", "Q4"), " exceeded targets")
```
